originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__n_9' during its initialization is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'n' during its initialization is never read
presenceCondition:
(or (and CONFIG_SLOB (not CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER) CONFIG_SLOB)
    (and (not CONFIG_SLOB) (not CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER) (not CONFIG_SLOB)))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SLOB () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 0f8f8094d28.c L39: Dead store of `n` (`int n = *cache` is assigned but never read). Not marked with ERROR — new bug. PC simplifies to !CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER (SLOB OR !SLOB factors out). The PC is under-constrained: (1) includes CONFIG_SLOB=T paths which are non-compilable in PRODUCT mode (kmalloc_caches undeclared when SLOB=T); (2) the code is inside #ifdef CONFIG_LOCKDEP so LOCKDEP=T is required but absent from PC. Real condition is LOCKDEP=T AND SLOB=F AND !FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER.
